speaker: OPERATOR

text: rejections in real time.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: It is a headless platform exposed through APIs, so it can integrate with existing first and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: third-party apps rather than running as a separate app.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our experience is one of the first areas of AI-powered transformation for enterprises.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Around the world, our conversations with three-level executives reflect how they view Adobe
speaker: OPERATOR

text: as the trusted partner for this transformation in the era of agentic AI.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: In Q2, we announced native integrations with major enterprise AI platforms, including Microsoft
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Co-pilot, Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google Gemini.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our partnership with NVIDIA brings CX Enterprise Co-worker capabilities into the Nemo Claw
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Enterprise Agent platform, enabling brands to deploy Adobe's customer experience intelligence
speaker: OPERATOR

text: within NVIDIA's secure policy-governed Open Shell runtime.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Leading global agencies, including Denzoo, Havas, Amnikam, Publises, Stagwell, and WPP,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: are standardizing on Adobe, combining our AI-powered capabilities with their unique
speaker: OPERATOR

text: IP and industry expertise to co-develop innovative, differentiated solutions for joint clients.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our vision, deep expertise in creativity and marketing, track record of innovation, and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: broad partner ecosystem uniquely position Adobe as the partner of choice for AI-powered
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customer experience orchestration.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our extensive customer base, innovative CXO products, and robust pipeline give us confidence
speaker: OPERATOR

text: for a seasonally strong second half.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I'll now pass it to Steve.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks, Anil.

text: Today, I will start by summarizing Adobe's performance in Q2 FY26.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Q2 growth drivers for creative and marketing professionals included.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Growth in creative cloud driven by the CC Pro offering.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Creative freemium Mal, which includes web and mobile versions of Firefly, Express, Premiere,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Photoshop, and Lightroom, across 90 million, growing over 70% year over year.

text: Continued strong generative credit consumption, driven by video and audio.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Firefly ending ARR, including Firefly apps and credit plans and enterprise Firefly offerings
speaker: OPERATOR

text: approaching 300 million, with the intent to drive more traffic to Firefly freemium in H2.

speaker: OPERATOR

text: year over year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Previous customers with over $10 million in ARR growing greater than 20% year over year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And continued strength in retention across the enterprise customer base.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Let me now turn to our financial targets, which include SEMrush and assume current macro
speaker: OPERATOR

text: economic conditions.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Given strong year to date performance, we are raising full year revenue and non-gap
speaker: OPERATOR

text: EPS targets.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For FY26, we are targeting total Adobe revenue of $26.5 to $26.6 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Business professionals and consumers subscription revenue of $7.44 to $7.48 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Creative and marketing professional subscription revenue of $18.21 to $18.27 billion, which
speaker: OPERATOR

text: now includes approximately $280 million from SEMrush.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Total Adobe ending ARR book of business growth of 10.2% year over year compared to our FY26
speaker: OPERATOR

text: beginning book of business of $25.66 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: App EPS of $17.90 to $18 and non-gap EPS of $24.35 to $24.45.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our FY26 targets assume a non-gap operating margin of approximately 45%.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: A gap tax rate of approximately 22.5% and a non-gap tax rate of approximately 18%.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: FY26 total Adobe ARR growth target of 10.2% now reflects both the addition of the SEMrush
speaker: OPERATOR

text: book of business as well as the strategic choice to accelerate mow, premium growth and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: defer previously planned creative cloud line optimizations.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We believe this is the right long-term strategy to expand our customer base and strengthen
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the foundation for durable growth.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For Q3 FY26 we are targeting total Adobe revenue of $6.67 to $6.72 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Business professionals and consumers subscription revenue of $1.87 to $1.89 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Government marketing professional subscription revenue of $4.61 to $4.64 billion.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Gap EPS of $4.40 to $4.45 and non-gap EPS of $6.05 to $6.10.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: For Q3 we assume non-gap operating margin of approximately 44% and a gap tax rate of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: approximately 23% and a non-gap tax rate of approximately 18%.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We believe Adobe is well positioned to capitalize on the expanding AI opportunity.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our focus remains on helping customers achieve better outcomes through innovation, relentless
speaker: OPERATOR

text: execution and deep integration of AI across our portfolio.
speaker: OPERATOR

speaker: OPERATOR
